title: Business Hosting in Japan: CPI, XServer, XServer for WordPress and ConoHa
metaTitle: Japan Business Hosting: CPI, XServer and XServer for WordPress
description: Compare CPI, XServer Business, XServer for WordPress, and ConoHa WING Business by price, email, WordPress operations, backup, SLA, and support.
lead: The shortlist changes depending on whether website and email hosting should remain integrated or WordPress operations should move to a specialized platform.
author: Luqevora Editorial Team

sources.label: CPI Business Standard pricing and onboarding
ConoHa WING Business plan official page

sections.heading: Conclusion: workflow, support and budget produce different shortlists
body: CPI is strongest when a team needs SmartRelease, separated domain environments, and Japanese business email. XServer Business is strongest when capacity, remote backups, migration support, and administrator controls are priorities. ConoHa WING Business is relevant to smaller organizations that want allocated vCPU and memory at a lower starting price with no setup fee.
Estimate website count, mail storage, recovery objectives, administrative roles, and technical staffing before comparing plans.
bullets: CPI for controlled release and multi-domain email operations
XServer for WordPress for staging, site access, and centralized WordPress operations
XServer Business for capacity, migration and administration
ConoHa WING Business for allocated resources and lower entry cost

sections.heading: Published plan characteristics
body: The table summarizes representative business plans at the verification date. Contract length and temporary promotions can change the displayed price.

rows: Representative starting price
JPY 4,840 monthly equivalent for twelve months
Shared Standard from JPY 3,762 monthly on a long term, before temporary campaign calculations
Promotional Basic from JPY 2,059 monthly on thirty-six months
Biz Light up to JPY 1,815 monthly on standard hourly billing, with long-term discounts
Setup fee
No charge for twelve months
JPY 16,500 for shared hosting
No setup fee on Basic and Standard
No charge
Storage
300 GB primary and 100 GB per multi-domain environment
700 GB NVMe on shared Standard
500 GB to 4 TB NVMe
700 GB Biz Light and 1,000 GB Biz Standard
Backup
Combined 10 GB web and database, up to 30 generations
Web, mail and MySQL for 14 days with remote storage
Managed WordPress backup and restore features
Confirm current backup scope and restore conditions in the detailed plan
Operational focus
SmartRelease, email authentication, separated domains
Migration, setup assistance and administrator users
WP Panel, staging, and subaccounts
Allocated vCPU and memory by plan

sections.heading: When CPI is the better shortlist
body: CPI is relevant when staging and controlled release are part of the publishing process, when multiple domains need separated environments, or when Japanese business email controls are important.
Check the standard 10 GB SmartRelease backup allowance, the limited SLA scope, and the paid 24-hour phone support option.

sections.heading: When XServer Business is the better shortlist
body: XServer Business is relevant when the organization wants large shared-server capacity, fourteen days of web, mail and database backups, configuration assistance, website migration, and administrator-user controls.
Separate temporary cashback pricing from regular contract costs and include the setup fee in the first-year total.

sections.heading: When XServer for WordPress is the better shortlist
body: It fits companies and agencies that host business email elsewhere and want to standardize multiple WordPress sites through WP Panel, staging, and site-specific subaccounts.
It does not provide mailboxes, so include a separate email service in total cost.

sections.heading: When ConoHa WING Business is the better shortlist
body: ConoHa WING Business publishes Biz Light, Biz Standard and Biz Advanced plans with allocated vCPU and memory. Standard billing is hourly with a monthly cap, no setup fee, and no minimum term.
The discounted WING Pack uses prepaid fixed terms and does not allow cancellation during the selected term. Review the domain benefit and term commitment together.

sections.heading: Pre-purchase checklist
body: Share the following requirements among the website owner, agency, finance team and IT administrator.
bullets: First-year and renewal total including setup and domains
Backup scope for web, email and databases
Restore responsibility and recovery time objective
Administrator roles for internal teams and agencies

faqs.question: What should a company compare first?
faqs.answer: Compare total cost, backup scope, restore procedures, SLA coverage, migration assistance, administrator controls, and support hours against the company operating process.

faqs.question: Is the lowest monthly price automatically the best choice?
faqs.answer: No. Migration work, downtime, restore labor, email interruption, and staff time can outweigh a small monthly price difference.

faqs.question: What matters when an agency manages the site?
faqs.answer: Document staging, release permission, multi-site separation, backup, recovery, DNS changes, contract ownership, and payment responsibility.
